evals.prompt: Build and ship this feature end to end.
evals.expected_output: Should route to ship-it. Should freeze the spec (or grill raw intent through the FREEZE gate), decompose into tracer-bullet slices, build each slice with tests, run acceptance review + runtime-prove, land to BASE, and advance through the release state machine as authorized. Should not route to clean-sweep or map-it.
assertions: Mentions decompose → build → review → prove → land → release
Names terminal states BUILT / PROMOTION_READY / RELEASED / DEPLOYED_AND_VERIFIED
Does not route to clean-sweep or map-it

evals.prompt: We have a frozen spec for a new billing API. Implement it.
evals.expected_output: Should enter ship-it at the frozen-spec branch (VALIDATE → DECOMPOSE), skip grilling, and run the canonical pipeline. Should produce a TRACEABILITY table mapping frozen acceptance criteria to passing tests on the BASE head.
assertions: Produces or references a TRACEABILITY table
Verifies acceptance criteria on BASE head
Does not re-grill a frozen spec

evals.prompt: Close every issue in the tracker.
evals.expected_output: Should NOT route to ship-it. Should identify this as a finite backlog drain and route to clean-sweep. Ship-it is for building new work, not closing an existing enumerated set.
assertions: Cites backlog / issue tracker as clean-sweep source
